compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
The delivered epic (Gerrit stack 558/560/561/563/576/579/580/581, NOT landed) diverged
from the APPROVED epic design on the authorship-payload format. bde5 (change 561,
commit c31fc7eb5) signs the event's RAW canonical bytes; the approved epic AC requires an
**in-toto Statement** payload whose `subject` binds `{event_uuid, content_hash}`. This
story transitions the authorship *machinery* to the approved design. (User rejected the
delivered design; full divergence record in session_log d7c0.)

## Stack scoping (why this story is authorship.py ONLY)
This story AMENDS Gerrit change 561 = commit c31fc7eb5 (bde5), which touches
`src/rebar/attest/authorship.py` and NOTHING else. At that commit the authorship
functions have **no consumers yet**: the write-time signing call site (`_seam.py`), the
merge-gate content-binding (`verify_authorship.py`), and the SNAPSHOT ledger
(`compact.py`) are all introduced later in commit 721702e91 (3183). Therefore the
consumer cutover to the in-toto payload — and the epic-AC-named `create_placeholder`
alias (which wraps `ensure_identity_for`, introduced in commit ec3aff507 / 2f13) — is
handled in **TS3 (117b, change 580)**, the story that reworks that commit. Keeping the
edits in the commit where the symbols actually exist is what keeps every intermediate
patchset internally green.

## What (authorship.py machinery)
Add the in-toto Statement layer as NEW functions, and PRESERVE the existing low-level
primitive so KEY-op signing (`identity.py._verify_keyop_signature`, which signs
`keyop_payload` bytes, not events) and any other caller keep compiling unchanged:

- KEEP `sign_authorship(payload, key_path, principal)` and `verify_authorship(envelope,
  identity_id)` as the low-level SSHSIG-over-DSSE primitive (used by KEY-op signatures).
- `authorship_content_hash(event: dict) -> str`: **SHA-256, lowercase hex** over the
  event's canonical JSON with the `author_sig` key EXCLUDED (the signature never covers
  itself).
- `build_authorship_statement(event_uuid, content_hash) -> dict`: an in-toto Statement:
  - `_type`: `"https://in-toto.io/Statement/v1"`
  - `subject`: `[{ "name": "<event_uuid>", "digest": { "sha256": "<content_hash>" } }]`
  - `predicateType`: `"application/vnd.rebar.authorship.v1+json"`
  - `predicate`: `{}`
- `sign_event_authorship(event: dict, key_path, principal) -> dsse.Envelope`: computes the
  content_hash, builds the Statement, and signs its canonical JSON bytes via the primitive
  (DSSE payloadType `application/vnd.rebar.authorship.v1+json`, PAE-signed under the pinned
  authorship namespace). The Statement JSON is the DSSE payload.
- `verify_event_authorship(event: dict, envelope, identity_id, *, repo_root=None) ->
  Verdict`: (1) parse the DSSE payload as an in-toto Statement, (2) check
  `subject[0].name == event['uuid']` AND `subject[0].digest.sha256 ==
  authorship_content_hash(event)`, (3) THEN verify the DSSE signature against the identity's
  in-band keys (reusing `resolve_trust_root`). Any parse/shape failure or subject/hash
  mismatch yields a non-verified Verdict; it NEVER raises on a lookup problem (mirrors the
  existing fail-closed contract).

## Scope
In: the in-toto Statement authorship machinery in `src/rebar/attest/authorship.py`
(content_hash + Statement build + `sign_event_authorship` + `verify_event_authorship`),
with the low-level `sign_authorship`/`verify_authorship` primitive preserved for KEY-ops.
Out: the write-seam stamping + merge-gate content-binding + SNAPSHOT ledger cutover to the
new payload AND the `create_placeholder` alias (all TS3/3183, commit 721702e91); the
era/validity model (TS2/e165); docs/ADR (TS4/b134).

## Landing / sequencing
TS1–TS4 land as ONE ATOMIC Gerrit stack submit (relation chain), so there is no runtime
window where a reworked commit is live without the others. Write-time signing is opt-in
and the stack is unlanded, so no persisted event carries an old-format `author_sig` — no
migration/backfill. Docs/ADR for this pivot are rewritten in TS4.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `sign_event_authorship` produces a DSSE envelope whose payload is an in-toto
  Statement (`_type` https://in-toto.io/Statement/v1, `predicateType`
  application/vnd.rebar.authorship.v1+json) with `subject[0] = {name: event_uuid,
  digest: {sha256: content_hash}}`; a test asserts the structure + payloadType.
- [ ] `authorship_content_hash` is SHA-256 hex over the canonical event with `author_sig`
  EXCLUDED; a test proves adding/removing an `author_sig` key does not change the hash,
  and mutating any other field DOES change it.
- [ ] `verify_event_authorship` returns verified for an event signed by identity A against
  A's in-band keys; a test asserts it does NOT verify against identity B, FAILS on a
  tampered event body (content_hash mismatch), and FAILS on a tampered signature.
- [ ] `verify_event_authorship` returns a non-verified Verdict (never raises) when the
  envelope payload is not a valid in-toto Statement (malformed / missing subject) and when
  the identity is unknown/keyless (test).
- [ ] The low-level `sign_authorship(payload,...)` primitive is unchanged: a KEY-op-style
  signature over `keyop_payload(...)` bytes still verifies via the primitive path (test),
  proving the Statement layer did not break KEY-op signing.

## file_impact
- `src/rebar/attest/authorship.py` (in-toto Statement build/parse + content_hash + event sign/verify; primitive preserved)

file_impact.reason: in-toto Statement build/parse + content_hash + event sign/verify; low-level primitive preserved for KEY-ops

compiled_state.title: transition: authorship payload -> in-toto Statement (subject binds {event_uuid, content_hash})
